behaviors.description: When a required key is absent from both the file and the environment, the error message names the missing key by its exact config path (e.g., `database.host`).
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff: file parsing + env overlay + required-key validation + error formatting. Unit tests of the validator alone can miss path-propagation bugs across those modules (MOCK-MISS). No real network/subprocess/AI dependency is involved, so higher levels are not required. Rejected System because the behavior text is about missing-key detection/formatting, not OS/subprocess/external API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run loader with a temp config file missing `database.host` and no corresponding `TOOL_...` env var; assert the raised startup error includes the exact dotted path `database.host`.

behaviors.description: When the environment variable `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set, the loader resolves the config file path relative to that directory rather than `~/.config`.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on wiring env reading into path-resolution logic, not just one pure function. An integration test catches miswiring between startup loader and path resolver. No real external API, subprocess, or model output is involved. Rejected System because behavior text only requires path selection from env, not OS-level event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Set `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` to a temp directory containing a known config file; invoke loader and assert it reads from `<XDG_CONFIG_HOME>/tool/config.yaml` and ignores `~/.config/tool/config.yaml`.

behaviors.description: When an environment variable with the `TOOL_` prefix conflicts with a key in the config file, the environment variable value is used.
behaviors.justification: This is precedence behavior across modules (YAML load + env extraction + merge policy). Unit tests on merge logic alone can miss key-mapping or prefix-handling integration bugs. No real infra/LLM pipeline dependency. Rejected System because no subprocess/network/external boundary contract is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create config with `database.host: file-host`, set `TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=env-host`, run loader, and assert effective config uses `env-host`.

behaviors.description: When the config file contains a YAML syntax error, the error message includes the line number where the problem was detected.
behaviors.justification: Need real parser + loader error translation together. A unit test with mocked parser can hide whether parser exceptions preserve line metadata through loader formatting (MOCK-HIDE). No AI dependency. Rejected System because behavior text targets YAML parse/format handling, not subprocess or network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide malformed YAML in a temp config file (with known bad line), invoke loader, and assert emitted error message includes that line number.

behaviors.description: When the config directory does not exist at startup, the loader creates it and writes a defaults file before proceeding.
behaviors.justification: This requires multi-module behavior with real local filesystem side effects (directory creation + file write + subsequent load). Integration level is sufficient because local filesystem verification is explicitly in scope there. Rejected System because the behavior does not require subprocess/network/external API contracts; local fs round-trip can be proven in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Start with a temp HOME/XDG path where `tool/` directory is absent; run loader; assert directory and default config file are created and then loader proceeds with valid effective config.
